denial_pattern_sources: wrong_benefit_category: CMS Essential Health Benefits Bulletin 2022; documented in Aetna member complaints
incorrect_demographic_guideline: Commonwealth Fund 2023 Review of Prior Auth Denials
denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

We received your provider's request on 07/03/2026 for Romiplostim (Nplate) related to Immune thrombocytopenia, chronic (D69.3). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines and Optum clinical criteria. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Romiplostim (Nplate) with an out-of-network provider is not covered without a gap exception. In-network alternatives are available.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with out of network no authorization. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.
clinical_context: The 67-year-old member has Immune thrombocytopenia, chronic (D69.3). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Romiplostim (Nplate) recommended as the next appropriate step. Platelets 18k despite steroids and IVIG trials. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that out of network no authorization bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.
